brief_document.title: Crate metadata and dependency audit for crates.io
brief_document.purpose: Add crates.io-required metadata to all 11 Aion crates and ensure inter-crate dependencies use version+path so cargo publish succeeds.
brief_document.task: For each crate in the workspace: add description, keywords, categories, and documentation fields to Cargo.toml. Ensure license, repository, edition, and rust-version inherit from workspace. Audit all inter-crate dependencies to use version = "0.1.0" alongside path references. Verify beamr is pinned to a published crates.io version. Run cargo publish --dry-run for every crate in dependency order.

requirements.title: Per-crate description and discovery metadata
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL have a description, keywords array, and categories array in every crate's Cargo.toml. Keywords SHALL include 'workflow', 'durable-execution', and crate-specific terms. Categories SHALL include 'development-tools' or 'asynchronous' as appropriate.
acceptance: All 11 crates have a non-empty description field.
All 11 crates have keywords and categories arrays.
cargo metadata --format-version 1 shows description for every workspace member.

requirements.title: Workspace inheritance verification
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL ensure every crate inherits license, repository, edition, and rust-version from the workspace package section.
acceptance: Every crate's Cargo.toml contains license.workspace = true.
Every crate's Cargo.toml contains repository.workspace = true.
cargo metadata confirms uniform license and repository across all members.

requirements.title: Inter-crate version+path dependencies
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL specify both version and path for every inter-crate dependency so that cargo publish resolves the version from crates.io while local development uses the path.
acceptance: Every dependency on a workspace crate uses { version = "0.1.0", path = "../crate-name" } syntax.
No dependency uses path-only without a version.
cargo publish --dry-run succeeds for every crate.

boundaries: SHALL add metadata to all 11 workspace crates.
SHALL verify beamr is available on crates.io at the pinned version.
SHALL NOT change any crate's public API or functionality.
SHALL NOT bump version numbers (stays at 0.1.0 for initial publish).
SHALL NOT actually publish to crates.io — dry-run only.
verification: cargo publish --dry-run succeeds for every crate in dependency order.
cargo metadata --format-version 1 shows description, license, and repository for every member.

checklist.text: Every crate has a description in Cargo.toml
Every crate has keywords and categories for crates.io discovery
Every crate inherits license, repository, edition, rust-version from workspace
Every crate has a documentation URL pointing to docs.rs
No path-only dependencies remain — all inter-crate deps use version + path
All external dependencies are available on crates.io
beamr dependency is pinned to a published crates.io version

stories.text: I can discover Aion crates on crates.io by searching for 'workflow engine' or 'durable execution'.
I can run cargo publish --dry-run on any crate to verify it's publishable.
I can verify that no crate has path-only dependencies that would prevent crates.io publication.
